title: Putin conducts a 'special musical operation' in Europe
subhead: Barred from the West, Valery Gergiev, maestro at the helm of Russia's two leading opera houses and cultural ambassador for Vladimir Putin, is seeking a return to conducting in Europe's major concert halls. In this cultural arena, Moscow is shifting from 'soft power' to 'sharp power.'

title: Netanyahu says Iran 'decimated,' as Tehran targets Gulf petro-facilities
Gaza: Phase two of Trump's peace plan stalls amid war with Iran
Umberto Bossi, provocative founder of Italy's League party, dies at 84
subhead: 'A passionate political leader and a sincere democrat,' Italian President Sergio Mattarella said in tribute on Thursday. Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani, head of the Forza Italia party, said Bossi had been a politician 'of great intelligence' and 'a key figure in bringing about change in Italy.'

title: Hegseth addresses cost of Iran war, says it 'takes money to kill bad guys'
subhead: Though the exact cost of the military operation against Iran has not yet been released, US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth indirectly confirmed the approximate value of the Pentagon's request for additional funding on Thursday, saying: "As far as $200 billion, I think that number could move."

title: Anne-Claire Legendre, new president of France's Arab World Institute: 'Our role is to provide tools for understanding'
subhead: Legendre, who replaced former culture minister Jack Lang in February, speaks to Le Monde about her priorities for revitalizing the institution.

title: Bertrand Badie, political scientist: 'Persian culture is an impetuous alliance of dignity and fury'
subhead: The international relations specialist, who is of Iranian descent on his father's side, speaks with Le Monde about the West's lack of understanding of Iran and its culture.

War in Iran: 'Europe said no to the US. This is new, and it is a major development'
subhead: The Europeans' refusal to join Washington in the current Middle East war shows that the transatlantic rift is deeper than the one seen during the 2003 Iraq crisis, observes Le Monde columnist Sylvie Kauffmann.

title: In Paris, Reza Pahlavi, son of Iran's last shah, builds his network
subhead: Reza Pahlavi has been based in the French capital since the US-Israeli strikes on Iran started, making moves in anticipation of the Islamic Republic's fall.

title: Violence in restaurant kitchens exposes deep wounds in fine dining
subhead: The recent allegations against renowned Danish chef René Redzepi highlight the urgent need for a profound overhaul of management practices in the restaurant industry. Initiatives are spreading in France to break the silence surrounding abuse in the sector.

title: Europeans' justified mistrust in Trump's war in Iran
'The war scripted by Trump is being tested by an Iranian regime determined to survive at any cost'
France's national political crisis is taking root locally
